text:
TYPE: CHAR 

LENGTH: 4 

SOURCE: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Death Index (NDI) 

VALUES: — 

COMMENT: All ICD-10 cause of death codes begin with an alpha character followed by two or three digits. NDI 
results do not include decimals in the cause of death codes. The decimal is implied between the 
second and third digits for ICD-10 codes. Researchers wishing to obtain this NDI segment of the MBSF 
must obtain an additional approval beyond the CMS DUA.
